Field notes on the survey tool.

The survey tool paints a heatmap of subsurface deposits before you commit to
harvesting. A dense survey reading means a rich node is nearby; a faint reading
means the deposit is thin or exhausted. Surveying costs a little energy but
saves a lot of wasted swings on poor nodes.

Deposit alignment matters. The survey heatmap and the actual harvest node must
agree, or the whole gathering loop feels broken. Always trust a fresh survey
over a stale one after the terrain has regenerated.
